data.title: Macquarie Upgrades XPeng Stock to Buy (XPEV) after Q1 Earnings; BofA Raises Price Target
data.description:
Chinese electric vehicle (EV) maker XPeng (XPEV) posted a loss for Q1 2026 due to sluggish sales, marking a reversal from its first-ever quarterly profit reported for the fourth quarter of 2025. However, the company's Q1 2026 gross margin improved significantly to 20.6% from 15.6% in the prior-year quarter. Also, XPeng issued an encouraging Q2 deliveries outlook in the range of 100,000 to 106,000 units, reflecting up to 69.1% sequential growth. Citing optimism about XPeng's new models, Macquarie analyst Eugene Hsiao upgraded XPeng stock to Buy from Hold with an unchanged price target of $19 and HK$73.

Memorial Day Sale – Claim 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock trusted, data-backed investing tools with TipRanks Premium, from analyst ratings and forecasts to breaking news and portfolio analysis.
  • Discover high-conviction stock picks and new investing opportunities with the TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter

Also, Bank of America analyst Ming Hsun Lee slightly raised his price target for XPEV stock and reiterated a Buy rating.

Macquarie Turns Bullish on XPEV Stock

Hsiao believes the worst of the volume decline is behind, given the robust order intake for XPeng's new GX model, which will be followed by the launch of the new MONA SUVs (L03/L05) and the larger G9L. Furthermore, the 4-star analyst sees potential upside from XPeng's future investments in humanoids and robotaxi.

Meanwhile, Hsiao increased his Fiscal 2026 unit volume guidance by 7% to reflect higher-than expected GX orders. Also, the analyst increased his gross margin estimate by 20 basis points on a better mix. However, Hsiao's Fiscal 2026 loss per share estimate now stands at -RMB 1.97, compared to the previous estimate of -RMB 1.28 due to higher research and development spending.

Hsiao increased his sales forecast, but slightly reduced his P/S target multiple to 1.3x FY26 sales estimate from 1.4x based on XPEV's EV and tech peer comparables.

BofA Stays Bullish on XPEV Stock

Bank of America's Lee slightly raised his price target, noting upbeat GX orders. The 4-star analyst highlighted that XPeng's Q1 results reflected rapidly growing overseas sales, which accounted for 20% of the overall revenue, with management targeting more than 10,000 monthly overseas unit sales by Q4 2026 and over 100% annual growth.

Looking ahead, Lee noted XPeng's Q2 2026 deliveries outlook in the 100,000 to 106,000 units. He added that the company expects its second-quarter gross margin to be essentially unchanged on a quarter-over-quarter basis, supported by better product mix due to the higher-margin GX model, partially offset by higher material costs.

Also, Lee highlighted that starting with GX, XPeng is launching four SUV models, which are expected to drive higher volumes in Q2 to Q4 2026. The analyst raised his 2026, 2027, and 2028 sales volume estimates by 5% each year, while adjusting his gross margin estimates by +1.3 percentage points, -0.3 percentage points, and -0.7 percentage points.

Overall, Lee remains bullish on XPeng as it expects new business, such as robotaxi and humanoid robots, to support its valuation.

Is XPEV a Good Stock to Buy?

Overall, Wall Street has a Moderate Buy consensus rating on XPeng stock based on eight Buys, two Holds, and one Sell. The average XPEV stock price target of $24 indicates 45.4% upside potential.
data.author: Sirisha Bhogaraju

data.title: How High Can Dell Technologies' Stock Price Rise?
data.description:
Dell Technologies (DELL) stock was on the rise Friday after the computer company reported earnings. The company's results were better than expected, and it also provided increased revenue guidance. Today's rally also had some investors wondering just how high DELL stock can rise. One expert believes DELL stock could hit $550.

Memorial Day Sale – Claim 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock trusted, data-backed investing tools with TipRanks Premium, from analyst ratings and forecasts to breaking news and portfolio analysis.
  • Discover high-conviction stock picks and new investing opportunities with the TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter

Five-star Barclays analyst Tim Long holds the Street-high price target for DELL stock. He boosted his price target to $550 today, which is more than threefold his previous price target of $168. This new price target represents a potential 34.61% upside. Long also maintained a Buy rating for the shares.

Why Is Long So Bullish on Dell Stock?

Long updated his price target for Dell stock after the company's latest earnings results. He cited Dell's strong performance as the reason for this price target increase. It wasn't just one business that triggered Long's price target boost, but rather all of Dell's operations. Even so, investors will note that Dell saw major gains in its AI server business during the quarter.

Dell's earnings report was strong enough that it had a positive effect on other computer stocks today. That includes Hewlett Packard Enterprise (HPE), which experienced a strong rally on Friday. Investors will also note that Hewlett Packard Enterprise is about to report earnings on Monday. It will be interesting to see if this earnings report can push HPE and DELL stock higher.

DELL Stock Movement Today

Dell stock was up 28.26% on Friday, extending a 226.22% year-to-date rally. The shares have also gained 184.94% over the past 12 months.

DELL stock trading activity today saw some 25.86 million shares change hands. That's well above the company's three-month average daily trading volume of about 6.68 million shares.

Is Dell Stock a Buy, Sell, or Hold?

Turning to Wall Street, the analysts' consensus rating for Dell is Moderate Buy, based on 14 Buy, five Hold, and one Sell rating over the past three months. With that comes an average DELL stock price target of $401.38, representing a potential 1.74% downside for the shares.
data.author: William White

data.title: "Paying for 2030 Economics at 2026 Prices": SpaceX IPO May Not Be For the Boomers
data.description:
For all those who have considered the upcoming SpaceX IPO and thought, finally, my chance to get in on the ground floor of a huge win to come, there may be reason to reconsider, depending on, of all things, your age. A new report out from The Information Briefing notes that those known as Baby Boomers may not get much out of the upcoming IPO.

Memorial Day Sale – Claim 70% Off TipRanks

Looking for exposure to SpaceX & Anthropic? Check out AGIX ETF

This might come as a shock, because Boomers have seen several decades of IPOs come and go. Some of them have led to staggering increases, the kind that make generational wealth happen. But The Information's Briefing noted that the SpaceX IPO makes sense for two classes of investor: day traders and younger investors. Boomers, meanwhile, could get "burned" by the process.

On the surface, that seems nuts. After all, there will be plenty of shares available to buy. Reports note that somewhere around 30% of SpaceX could be available for retail buyers. But this may mean extreme volatility, and the possibility that shares will trade near the top of valuation for a while. And there are certainly paths for SpaceX to grow into, and beyond, a hoped-for $2 trillion valuation. But Franco Granda, an analyst with Pitchbook, noted, "…investors should understand that they are paying for 2030 economics at 2026 prices."

And Then There's Tesla

Meanwhile, there are concerns that SpaceX will ultimately merge with Tesla. This is not out of line. After all, SpaceX has regular cash flow thanks to its Starlink internet service, and Tesla needs solid cash flow right now as it makes its move from cars to robots and energy. But Wedbush's Daniel Ives—who has a five-star rating on TipRanks—suggests that such a move could be bad for Tesla shareholders in the end.

There are some resources being shared between SpaceX and Tesla. SpaceX bought a bunch of Cybertrucks not so long ago, and with the Terafab chip project and the possibility of orbital data centers coming up, Tesla and SpaceX may have more overlap than some expect. But given that Elon Musk holds about 85% control of SpaceX, folding it into Tesla may simply give Musk more control of the whole entity.

Is Tesla a Buy, Hold or Sell?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Hold consensus rating on TSLA stock based on 12 Buys, 12 Holds, and five Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. After a 27.6% rally in its share price over the past year, the average TSLA price target of $403.86 per share implies 6.89% downside risk.
data.author: Steve Anderson

data.title: SpaceX Contractor Falls to Death at Starbase, Sheriff Rules It an Accident
data.description:
Jose Luis Bautista Jr., a contractor working at the rocket firm SpaceX's Starbase facility in Texas, has died after a heavy metal beam collapsed during construction operations. Investigators looked into the case and later ruled the incident was an accident, while federal workplace safety officials opened a separate investigation into the fatal event.

Memorial Day Sale – Claim 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock trusted, data-backed investing tools with TipRanks Premium, from analyst ratings and forecasts to breaking news and portfolio analysis.
  • Discover high-conviction stock picks and new investing opportunities with the TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter

SpaceX Contractor Dies After Beam Collapse at Starbase

According to the Cameron County Sheriff's Office, 25-year-old Bautista was working from a scissor lift during early morning construction activities, with his brother, at Starbase near Brownsville on May 15. The contractor was trying to replace the beams inside the building under renovation to support the almost 400-foot-tall Starship rockets built at the Starbase launch site. 

To achieve this, he was attached by harness to a roughly 58-foot metal beam weighing about 7,770 pounds when the structure suddenly tilted and collapsed. The falling beam had pulled him from the lift before he struck the setup and landed on the concrete floor below.

SpaceX's security team was notified right after the incident, which occurred around 4 a.m. Central Time. An emergency officer then attempted CPR before Bautista was transported to a medical center in Brownsville, where he was later pronounced dead.

OSHA Investigation Underway Following Accident

The sheriff's office has ruled Bautista's death as "accidental" after a doctor claimed the cause of death was blunt-force trauma. However, investigators said a site foreman believed Bautista may have assumed the beam he had attached himself to was securely anchored before it lost control.

Bautista worked for Delta Fabrication & Machine, a construction-services contractor running at the SpaceX facility. However, neither the company nor SpaceX has publicly commented on the incident.

Meanwhile, the Occupational Safety and Health Administration has launched an investigation into the death as part of a federal workplace safety review. At the same time, Starbase City Administrator Cayetana Polanco grieved with Bautista's family and loved ones in an email. However, she added that the aerospace giant's activities are not under the city's control. 

What Is the Best Aerospace Stock to Buy Now? 

While SpaceX remains a private company ahead of its highly anticipated June IPO, investors can still explore public companies with direct exposure to the aerospace industry. Wall Street analysts tracked rate-sensitive stocks such as Boeing Co (BA) and Howmet Aerospace Inc (HWM) as Strong Buys. Meanwhile, General Dynamics Corp (GD) and Northrop Grumman (NOC) are rated Moderate Buys. For more information on their performance, price targets, and ratings, visit the TipRanks Stocks Comparison Center.
data.author: Joy Iyke

data.title: Okta Stock (OKTA) Snags Price Target Boosts from Analysts After Earnings
data.description:
Okta (OKTA) stock received strong analyst praise on Friday. This was thanks to the identity solutions company's Q1 earnings report. This saw it beat Wall Street's adjusted earnings per share and revenue estimates. The company also provided solid guidance for the year in its earnings release. All of these positive catalysts triggered updated analyst coverage of OKTA stock.

Where Do Analysts Stand on Okta Stock After Earnings?

Mizuho Securities analyst Gregg Moskowitz was among those impressed by Okta's earnings. He reiterated a Buy rating for OKTA stock and raised his price target to $110 from $100. However, the stock has rallied higher than expected after earnings, meaning this price target represents a potential 6.58% downside for the shares.

Moskowitz highlighted Okta's solid Q1 earnings and "encouraging" outlook in his latest note to investors. The analyst also said that Okta remains a leader in the "critically important" identity management market. He also expects Okta to continue to benefit from new products.

OKTA stock also received updated price targets from several other analysts.

  • Wells Fargo analyst Richard Poland boosted his price target to $100 from $85.
  • UBS analyst Roger Boyd increased his price target to $130 from $115.
  • Raymond James analyst Adam Tindle raised his price target to $115 from $85.
  • RBC Capital analyst Matthew Hedberg boosted his price target to $122 from $108.

data.title: CoreWeave (CRWV) Board Member Sells $106 Million Worth of Company Stock
data.description:
A member of CoreWeave's (CRWV) board of directors has sold $106 million worth of company stock.

Memorial Day Sale – Claim 70% Off TipRanks

Explore CWVX for 2X leverage on CRWV

Director Jack Cogen sold 986,540 shares of CoreWeave stock on May 26 for total proceeds of $106.3 million. The shares were offloaded at prices ranging from $106.54 to $109.02 per share. The insider sales come as CoreWeave shares trade at $106.87, with the stock up 49% this year.

Cogen sold the CRWV stock through several trusts he controls. It's not clear how much CoreWeave stock he continues to own following the latest sale, which is dramatic given its size. Jack Cogen is a private investor who has served as a member of CoreWeave's board of directors since September 2017.
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: Why Is the Stock Market Up Today, 5/29/26?
data.description:
Both the S&P 500 (SPX) and the Nasdaq 100 (NDX) are trading higher on Friday amid growing optimism over a U.S.-Iran deal.

Memorial Day Sale – Claim 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock trusted, data-backed investing tools with TipRanks Premium, from analyst ratings and forecasts to breaking news and portfolio analysis.
  • Discover high-conviction stock picks and new investing opportunities with the TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter

Vice President J.D. Vance said the two countries are "very close" to inking a deal that would include reopening the Strait of Hormuz, extending the existing ceasefire by 60 days, and starting negotiations on restricting Iran's nuclear program. In addition, gas prices across the U.S. fell by 3.5 cents overnight, the largest one-day drop in 17 years.

Lower Energy Prices Cool Inflation Fears as Software Stocks Lead Gains

Falling gas prices have helped ease fears of inflation. On top of that, Brent crude (BZ) is set for its largest monthly drop since 2020.

Elsewhere, software stocks are pulling the market higher following several encouraging earnings reports within the sector. Oracle (ORCL), Palantir (PLTR), ServiceNow (NOW), and CrowdStrike (CRWD) are all up by over 5% at the time of writing.
data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: Why Penny Stock Replimune (REPL) Nearly Doubled Today
data.description:
Shares of Replimune (REPL) are surging today after the biotech company announced that it plans to resubmit its melanoma drug RP1 for FDA approval after the treatment was rejected twice by the agency. The company said it reached an agreement with the FDA to file again, thereby giving RP1 another shot at approval after a controversial review process. The drug, also known as vusolimogene oderparepvec, is being tested in combination with Bristol Myers Squibb's (BMY) Opdivo for advanced melanoma patients whose cancer has progressed after anti-PD-1 therapy.

Memorial Day Sale – Claim 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock trusted, data-backed investing tools with TipRanks Premium, from analyst ratings and forecasts to breaking news and portfolio analysis.
  • Discover high-conviction stock picks and new investing opportunities with the TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter

The FDA had previously rejected the application because Replimune relied on a single-arm study without a control group, which the agency said did not provide enough evidence of effectiveness. Notably, former FDA Commissioner Marty Makary had defended the rejection by saying the company needed stronger data from a well-controlled trial. Interestingly, though, the timing is important because the new resubmission comes after leadership changes at the FDA, including Makary's departure.

That shakeup has raised hopes among some biotech investors that Replimune may get a more favorable review this time. Still, approval is not guaranteed, since the central question remains whether the company can convince regulators that RP1 has enough evidence behind it. For Replimune, the resubmission gives the company a third chance to turn RP1 into an approved treatment for advanced melanoma.

Is REPL Stock a Good Buy?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Moderate Sell consensus rating on REPL stock based on four Holds and three Sells assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. Furthermore, the average REPL price target of $2.25 per share implies 73.3% downside risk.
data.author: Vince Condarcuri

data.title: ServiceNow Stock (NOW) Gets a Boost from Snowflake Earnings
data.description:
ServiceNow (NOW) stock was on the rise Friday as investors took interest in the cloud computing company thanks to its ties to Snowflake (SNOW). ServiceNow and Snowflake are partners in AI processing capabilities. This sees Snowflake pass on AI information from its network directly to ServiceNow's systems.

Memorial Day Sale – Claim 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock trusted, data-backed investing tools with TipRanks Premium, from analyst ratings and forecasts to breaking news and portfolio analysis.
  • Discover high-conviction stock picks and new investing opportunities with the TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter

Here's the thing. Snowflake just reported an incredibly strong earnings report, with adjusted earnings per share and revenue above Wall Street's estimates. This also triggered a wave of increased analyst price targets for SNOW stock and pushed the shares higher. With that strong momentum for SNOW stock, it makes sense that investors would also take note of ServiceNow due to the companies' connections.

However, it will still be several weeks before investors see if Snowflake's strong earnings have benefited ServiceNow. The company won't report earnings until July 29, 2026. When it does, traders will want to note its adjusted EPS and revenue to see how they compare to estimates. Wall Street expects adjusted EPS of 86 cents and revenue of $3.93 billion.

ServiceNow Stock Movement Today

ServiceNow stock was up roughly 11% on Friday, but was still down 21.17% year-to-date. The stock has also dropped 46.23% over the past 12 months.

Traders will note that ServiceNow stock trading activity today saw nearly 20 million shares traded. For comparison, the company's three-month average daily trading volume is about 29.05 million shares.

Is ServiceNow Stock a Buy, Sell, or Hold?

Turning to Wall Street, the analysts' consensus rating for ServiceNow is Strong Buy, based on 35 Buy and four Hold ratings over the past three months. With that comes an average NOW stock price target of $141.68, representing a potential 16.75% upside for the shares.
data.author: William White

data.title: Trump's 'Anti-Weaponization Fund' Halted by Federal Judge
data.description:
President Trump's "anti-weaponization fund" ran into a roadblock on Friday after a federal judge in Virginia blocked his plan to create a $1.776 billion fund for alleged victims of politically motivated enforcement actions.

Memorial Day Sale – Claim 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock trusted, data-backed investing tools with TipRanks Premium, from analyst ratings and forecasts to breaking news and portfolio analysis.
  • Discover high-conviction stock picks and new investing opportunities with the TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter

U.S. District Judge Leonie Brinkema said the Trump administration is barred from taking any actions related to the fund, including "the transferring of money to the Fund; the consideration of any claims submitted to the Fund; and the disbursing of any funds from the Fund."

Judge Sets June 12 Hearing as Fund Faces Pushback

Brinkema scheduled a hearing for June 12 to decide whether to extend the pause on the government's plan to create the fund, which has received steep pushback.

Earlier this month, the Justice Department said the Trump Organization would withdraw its $10 billion lawsuit against the IRS as part of a deal to establish an "anti-weaponization fund." The case stemmed from allegations that an IRS employee leaked Trump's 2019 and 2020 tax records.
data.author: Eddie Pan
